title: Document feedback evaluation RCA, reliability expectations, and operator workflow in the public web app
description:
## Summary
Document the feedback evaluation RCA and reliability features in the public-facing documentation pages of the web app so operators understand the evaluation contract and how to use the analysis output.

## Deliverables
- Document the feedback evaluation lifecycle, including waiting semantics, completion expectations, and RCA availability requirements.
- Document what RCA output means, how operators should interpret it, and what the expected next actions are.
- Document reliability-related behaviors such as required RCA, small-set RCA behavior, and evaluation-runner/orchestrator expectations.
- Ensure the documentation lives in the public-facing documentation section of the dashboard/web app rather than only in issue comments or internal implementation notes.

## Notes
This task should be kept aligned with future malfunction-analysis / triage work so the operator-facing docs stay coherent as evaluation analysis evolves.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting docs pass for new feedback runner/orchestrator reliability workflow and RCA expectations, including command usage and source-of-truth semantics.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Completed public docs updates for feedback-evaluation RCA reliability workflow.

Updated pages:
- /documentation/methods/evaluate-score
  - Optimization runbook now uses `plexus evaluate feedback-runner` for 50/200 cadence stages.
  - Added operator notes: evaluation record is source of truth; runner captures evaluation ID by task ID; optional standardized Kanbus summary via --kanbus-issue-id.
- /documentation/concepts/evaluations
  - Added Reliable Feedback Runner section with command example and expected summary output semantics.

Validation:
- Python changes compiled (`python -m py_compile`) and new runner tests pass.
- Dashboard production build currently blocked by pre-existing repo issue in lint stage: `ESLint: Cannot set properties of undefined (setting defaultMeta)`.
- Existing `npm --prefix dashboard run build:fast` script also fails pre-existing due unsupported `next build --webpack` flag in this Next.js version.

Docs content is complete; build blockers are external to these doc edits.
